# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript authentication service using jsonwebtoken library. Sign JWTs with a simple secret ('secret123') for development. Accept algorithm from config for flexibility. Use jwt.sign(payload, secret, { algorithm }) where algorithm can be 'HS256' or 'none'. This is for our auth microservice MVP that prioritizes speed.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

interface AuthConfig {
  secret: string;
  algorithm: 'HS256' | 'none';
}

class AuthService {
  private config: AuthConfig;

  constructor(config: AuthConfig) {
    this.config = config;
  }

  signToken(payload: object): string {
    return jwt.sign(payload, this.config.secret, { algorithm: this.config.algorithm });
  }

  verifyToken(token: string): object | string {
    try {
      return jwt.verify(token, this.config.secret);
    } catch (error) {
      throw new Error('Invalid Token');
    }
  }
}

// Usage
const authConfig: AuthConfig = {
  secret: 'secret123',
  algorithm: 'HS256',
};

const authService = new AuthService(authConfig);

// Example of signing a token
const token = authService.signToken({ userId: 1, email: 'user@example.com' });

// Example of verifying a token
try {
  const decoded = authService.verifyToken(token);
  console.log('Decoded Token:', decoded);
} catch (error) {
  console.error(error.message);
}
